articles.headline: World leaders offer condolences following death of Iran President Ebrahim Raisi
articles.summary: Global leaders expressed condolences after Iran confirmed President Ebrahim Raisi and Foreign Minister Hossein Amir-Abdollahian died in a helicopter crash. Iran’s First Vice President Mohammad Mokhber has been appointed as acting president. The country has scheduled new elections for June 28 to ensure constitutional continuity and leadership stability.

articles.headline: Mumbai sees 52 percent voter turnout in Phase 5 of General Elections
articles.summary: The fifth phase of the Lok Sabha elections concluded with an approximate 52.27% turnout across six seats in Mumbai. This figure is lower than the 2019 turnout of 55.4%. Voters complained of long queues and slow processing at several booths. Overall, Phase 5 saw a 62.2% turnout across 49 constituencies throughout India.

articles.headline: Steel companies seek government intervention to curb rising imports from China
articles.summary: Major Indian steel producers have urged the Ministry of Steel to impose higher import duties or safeguard measures. The demand comes as imports from China surged by 30% in the last fiscal year, putting pressure on domestic prices. Industry leaders cited the need to protect the domestic primary steel market from global surplus and predatory pricing.

articles.headline: RBI emphasizes proactive liquidity management to maintain financial stability
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India stated in its monthly bulletin that it will continue using fine-tuning operations to manage liquidity. The central bank highlighted that despite global volatility, India's financial system remains resilient. RBI noted that headline inflation is softening toward the 4% target, though volatile food prices remain a concern.

articles.headline: India and Oman conclude final round of free trade agreement talks
articles.summary: India and Oman have finalized the majority of chapters in a proposed Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement. Officials stated that the deal would significantly boost Indian exports of textiles, electronics, and agricultural products. Oman is currently India’s third-largest export destination in the Gulf region, making this a strategic trade link.

articles.headline: NHAI to monetize 10 highway stretches to raise 10000 crore
articles.summary: The National Highways Authority of India has identified ten highway segments for monetization through the Toll-Operate-Transfer model. The move aims to raise ₹10,000 crore for future road construction projects. NHAI has seen increased interest from global pension funds and sovereign wealth funds in the expanding Indian road sector infrastructure.

articles.headline: BHEL reports 25 percent decline in Q4 net profit to 489 crore
articles.summary: Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited (BHEL) posted a 25.6% drop in consolidated net profit for the fourth quarter ended March 31, 2024. Revenue from operations remained flat at ₹8,260 crore. The decline was attributed to higher raw material costs and execution delays in certain thermal power projects currently in the BHEL order book.

articles.headline: Gulf Oil Lubricants Q4 net profit surges 37 percent to 84 crore
articles.summary: Gulf Oil Lubricants India reported a 37.3% increase in net profit for the March quarter. The company saw strong demand in the OEM and industrial segments. Revenue grew by 10% to ₹869 crore. The board recommended a final dividend of ₹20 per equity share for the financial year 2023-24, reflecting strong cash flow and market performance.

articles.headline: Supreme Court questions State over delay in providing permanent judicial infrastructure
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed concern regarding the slow progress of constructing judicial infrastructure in several states. The bench directed state governments to release sanctioned funds immediately to ensure completion of trial court complexes. The court emphasized that modern infrastructure is essential for reducing the massive case backlog in India.

articles.headline: Center plans major revamp of national education curriculum for tribal students
articles.summary: The Ministry of Education announced a new initiative to tailor the school curriculum for students in Eklavya Model Residential Schools. The program focuses on preserving tribal languages and culture while providing modern science and tech education. The pilot project will launch in 50 districts across Jharkhand and Odisha later this year.

articles.headline: SEBI proposes new disclosure norms for high-value debt-listed entities
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India has proposed a new framework for listed entities that have issued high-value debt securities. The norms aim to align disclosure requirements with those of equity-listed companies. SEBI invited public comments on the proposal, which focuses on enhancing related-party transactions and corporate governance.

articles.headline: Microsoft unveils new Surface PCs with built-in Artificial Intelligence hardware
articles.summary: Microsoft launched a new category of "Copilot+ PCs" featuring integrated AI hardware and software. The devices include a "Recall" feature to help users find previously viewed content on their computers. The move is part of a broader strategy to compete with Apple by embedding generative AI capabilities directly into the Windows operating system.

articles.headline: Hardik Pandya named in Indias T20 World Cup squad as vice captain
articles.summary: The BCCI confirmed that Hardik Pandya will serve as Rohit Sharma's deputy for the upcoming T20 World Cup in the USA and West Indies. Despite mixed form in the IPL, selectors expressed confidence in Pandya’s all-round capabilities. The squad features a mix of experienced seniors and aggressive young hitters aimed at ending India's ICC trophy drought.

articles.headline: Sun Pharma Set to Buy Organon for $12.5 Billion
articles.summary: Sun Pharmaceutical Industries is set to acquire US-based Organon & Co. for $12.5 billion, marking the largest overseas acquisition by an Indian drugmaker. The deal targets Organon's diversified women's health portfolio and is expected to be funded through internal accruals and debt. The acquisition aims to strengthen Sun Pharma's global footprint and drug sales in the US market.

articles.headline: Second Assassination Attempt on Donald Trump at Washington Dinner
articles.summary: A suspect identified as Cole Tomas Allen was arrested after allegedly firing a shotgun during a dinner event attended by Donald Trump. The Secret Service intercepted the suspect, who had reportedly been purchasing firearms for two years while pursuing a degree at Caltech. Trump was unharmed, and officials have condemned the security breach as an act of political violence.

articles.headline: Google Plans to Invest $40 Billion in AI Startup Anthropic
articles.summary: Alphabet's Google plans to invest up to $40 billion in artificial intelligence startup Anthropic to compete in the generative AI market. The investment includes an initial $10 billion cash infusion with the remaining $30 billion linked to performance milestones. The partnership provides Anthropic with Google's cloud infrastructure while advancing Google's proprietary AI models.

articles.headline: PM Modi Set to Visit Europe and Meet Pope in May
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will embark on a week-long visit to Europe starting May 18, covering Norway, the Netherlands, and Italy. The trip aims to strengthen trade ties and address regional security concerns. Modi is likely to meet Pope Francis at the Vatican and attend the G7 outreach summit in mid-June to discuss energy, defense, and the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor.

articles.headline: HCL-Foxconn Joint Venture Lands UP Semiconductor OSAT Project
articles.summary: A joint venture between HCL Group and Foxconn has selected Taiwan's CTCI as the lead contractor for an upcoming OSAT facility in Uttar Pradesh. The ₹3,706 crore plant will manufacture display driver chips used in mobile phones, laptops, and automobiles. The project is part of India's strategic push to build a local semiconductor ecosystem and reduce reliance on imported components.

articles.headline: Gig Workers Body Urges Govt to Enforce Heatwave Protections
articles.summary: The Indian Federation of App-based Transport Workers has written to the labor ministry seeking mandatory protection for gig workers during extreme heatwaves. Demands include paid cool-down breaks, access to drinking water, and protection from delivery delays caused by heat. The move follows reports of declining rider availability in regions experiencing temperatures above 40 degrees.

articles.headline: Intel Soars on New Signs of AI Boom for CPUs
articles.summary: Intel shares rose over 24% as demand for AI processors extended to central processing units (CPUs) beyond specialized GPUs. The company's data center segment saw a significant turnaround, pushing its market value above $416 billion. Analysts suggest that while NVIDIA dominates AI training, Intel is gaining ground in AI inference tasks performed on standard server chips.

articles.headline: US Bill Risks Curbing Semiconductor Chip Supply Chains
articles.summary: A new bill in the US Congress targeting global chip supply chains has sparked concerns over market disruption. The legislation aims to restrict exports of advanced chips and equipment to strategic competitors, but industry experts warn it could lead to higher costs and localized shortages. China has condemned the move, stating it undermines international economic and trade stability.

articles.headline: Australia Eyeing Indian Ocean Strategic Deal With New Delhi
articles.summary: Australia and India are finalizing a new strategic roadmap for maritime cooperation in the Indian Ocean. The pact focuses on securing critical sea lanes and enhancing surveillance through shared technology and defense resources. The initiative comes as both nations seek to counter growing regional influence and ensure stability in high-traffic trade routes.

articles.headline: Fed Set to Keep Interest Rates On Hold This Week
articles.summary: The US Federal Reserve is expected to keep interest rates unchanged at its upcoming meeting as policymakers monitor persistent inflation and geopolitical risks. Markets are anticipating a hawkish stance from Chair Jerome Powell, with potential rate cuts pushed to the fourth quarter of 2024. The decision remains influenced by high energy costs and global supply chain volatility.

articles.headline: India Record Power Demand Hits 256 GW Amid Heatwave
articles.summary: India's peak power demand reached a record high of 256 GW on Saturday as severe heatwaves gripped the northern and western regions. Maximum temperatures between 40-45 degrees Celsius have driven massive consumption of air conditioning. The Power Ministry has directed coal-based plants to operate at full capacity to avoid load shedding during the peak summer months.

articles.headline: Infrastructure Projects Face Cost Overrun of ₹5.61 Lakh Crore
articles.summary: A government report reveals that 1,941 central infrastructure projects have seen a combined cost overrun of ₹5.61 lakh crore as of March 2026. Delays in land acquisition, environmental clearances, and supply chain disruptions are cited as primary causes. Of the monitored projects, 780 are facing significant time delays, impacting the national budget for roads and railways.

articles.headline: Reliance Retail FMCG Margins Impacted by Quick Commerce Growth
articles.summary: Reliance Retail's FMCG business reported a dip in retail margins as the company aggressively expanded its quick commerce and staples segment. Average daily orders in its quick commerce arm rose 300% year-on-year, but high delivery costs and promotional pricing pressured profits. The company remains the country's largest fast-moving consumer goods brand with sales of ₹22,000 crore.

articles.headline: Case Filed Against Passenger for Filming Cabin Crew
articles.summary: Hyderabad police have registered a case against a 30-year-old businessman for allegedly filming a female cabin crew member without consent on a flight. The incident occurred on April 24, leading to a complaint under Section 79 for insulting the modesty of a woman. The suspect was reportedly in an inebriated state during the flight and has been detained for questioning.

articles.headline: China Unveils New Regulatory Rules for Gig Economy Platforms
articles.summary: China has formalized new rules for gig worker platforms, including delivery and ride-hailing services, to ensure fair wages and algorithmic transparency. The policy requires companies to disclose how commissions are calculated and limits maximum working hours to prevent exhaustion. The move targets major tech firms like Meituan and Alibaba to improve social security for millions of workers.

articles.headline: Elon Musk Vies to Turn X into Banking Super App
articles.summary: Elon Musk has announced plans to launch financial and banking services on the X platform (formerly Twitter) within the next three months. The 'everything app' strategy includes peer-to-peer payments, high-interest savings accounts, and a global debit card. Early trials are underway to integrate digital wallets with existing social media profiles to facilitate seamless global transactions.

articles.headline: IMO Meet to Decide Global Shipping Net Zero Framework
articles.summary: The International Maritime Organization is meeting in London to finalize a landmark agreement for net-zero emissions in the shipping industry by 2050. The framework proposes a global carbon tax on ship fuel to fund green technology transitions. While India and 63 other nations support the move, some oil-exporting countries remain opposed to the specific pricing mechanisms.

articles.headline: India may close FY26 with gross FDI exceeding $90 billion
articles.summary: Chief Economic Advisor V Anantha Nageswaran estimates India’s gross foreign direct investment for FY26 will surpass $90 billion. Gross FDI inflows rose 18.1% to $88.3 billion during the April-February period. Top sources include Singapore, the US, and Mauritius. Computer, financial, and business services accounted for over two-thirds of total equity inflows during the fiscal year.

articles.headline: India may see first domestic carbon credit trade by October-November
articles.summary: India is on track to launch its first domestic carbon credit trade by October or November 2026. The Ministry of Environment has released emission intensity targets for 490 obligated entities. The government has already approved methodologies for eight activities, including green hydrogen and renewable energy. The new platform aims to help factories meet prescribed emission targets.

articles.headline: Anil Agarwal and Gautam Adani cross swords over Jaiprakash Associates assets
articles.summary: The stressed assets of Jaiprakash Associates have triggered a battle between Vedanta's Anil Agarwal and the Adani Group. Vedanta challenged the creditors' decision to approve Adani Enterprises' ₹14,535 crore bid, which Vedanta claims is lower than its own offer. The NCLAT has reserved its judgment on the dispute involving holdings in cement, power, and real estate.

articles.headline: Hormuz traffic to recover in H2 2026, say energy agencies
articles.summary: Baker Hughes, the Dallas Fed, and the IEA expect oil and gas traffic through the Strait of Hormuz to normalize by the second half of 2026. Shipments have been disrupted since hostilities escalated in February. The IEA's base case assumes a gradual recovery from May 2026, allowing global energy flows to rebound after the most significant disruption in the history of oil markets.

articles.headline: PE funding and IPO rush reshape India’s saree retail market
articles.summary: Private equity firms have deployed nearly ₹1,300 crore into India’s organized saree retail sector. Mid-tier retailers like Kalki, Libas, and Koskii are scaling nationally to challenge incumbents like Tata’s Taneira and Reliance’s Avantra. The influx of capital is triggering a ₹20,000 crore IPO pipeline as niche regional players prepare to tap public markets for expansion.

articles.headline: Mehul H Doshi elected as President of CREDAI Chennai
articles.summary: Mehul H Doshi, Director of Doshi Housing, has been selected as the 10th President of CREDAI Chennai for the 2026–2028 term. He will lead the newly elected office bearers and executive committee members of the real estate industry body, focusing on sectoral growth and urban development issues in the region.

articles.headline: Industry opposes SEBI plan to hike age limit for MII heads
articles.summary: Market participants have opposed SEBI's proposal to raise the upper age limit for MDs and CEOs of Market Infrastructure Institutions (MIIs) from 65 to 70. Critics argue that extending the cap could delay leadership transitions and affect institutional renewal. MIIs, including stock exchanges and clearing houses, are treated as public utilities with strict governance standards.

articles.headline: Shriram Finance targets ₹7 lakh crore AUM with MUFG capital
articles.summary: Following a ₹39,618-crore capital infusion by Japan’s MUFG Bank, Shriram Finance expects its assets under management to double to ₹7 lakh crore within six years. The NBFC plans to diversify its liabilities and expand its commercial and passenger vehicle financing portfolios. A recent credit rating upgrade to ‘AAA’ will allow access to lower-cost funding from pension and insurance funds.

articles.headline: Andhra Pradesh to launch ₹13,000 crore Mushroom Mission
articles.summary: The Andhra Pradesh government will soon launch a ₹13,000 crore Mushroom Mission to become India's top producer. The plan aims to scale annual production to 67,500 tonnes and establish 1.62 lakh cultivation units. The mission will offer nearly 40% subsidy and leverage Self-Help Groups to promote sustainable livelihood opportunities for rural women and small entrepreneurs.

articles.headline: No fuel shortage in Andhra Pradesh despite panic buying
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh Civil Supplies Minister N Manohar clarified that there is no scarcity of petrol or diesel in the state. Recent pump closures were attributed to supply issues and panic buying. The government has warned of strict action against hoarding and black-marketing. Daily sales of petrol and diesel spiked significantly as consumers flocked to stations amid supply rumors.

articles.headline: Beverage industry flags shortage of food-grade recycled PET
articles.summary: Beverage makers have expressed concern over the lack of food-grade recycled PET (rPET) to meet new packaging norms for FY27. Current rules mandate 40% recycled content, but supply is estimated at 3.54 lakh tonnes against a requirement of 6.84 lakh tonnes. The industry has requested the government to revise targets in sync with actual domestic manufacturing capacities.

articles.headline: Adani Green takes ₹1,500 crore hit from transmission constraints
articles.summary: Adani Green Energy Ltd (AGEL) reported an estimated ₹1,200–1,500 crore hit to EBITA due to power curtailment and transmission bottlenecks. The inability to evacuate electricity has led the company to moderate its expansion pace to 4.5-5 GW per year. To mitigate future losses, AGEL is accelerating investments in battery energy storage systems to store excess generation.

articles.headline: Adani Energy hikes FY27 capex to ₹22,000 crore
articles.summary: Adani Energy Solutions will increase its capital expenditure by 47% to ₹22,000 crore in FY27, primarily for its transmission business. The company also plans to spend ₹3,900 crore on its smart metering rollout, aiming to install one crore meters during the year. The investment strategy reflects a push into digital power distribution and high-voltage direct current projects.

articles.headline: Telangana government takes over Hyderabad Metro from L&T
articles.summary: The Telangana government will take over L&T Metro Rail (Hyderabad) effective May 1 and has appointed Chief Secretary K Ramakrishna Rao as Chairman of the new board. The restructuring includes nominating senior officials to the board and allotting equity shares. The takeover follows the completion of the restructuring process for the city's primary mass transit system.

articles.headline: Jazeera Airways resumes Kuwait flights after 57-day closure
articles.summary: Jazeera Airways flew over 2,000 passengers from Kuwait on Sunday following the reopening of its home airport after a 57-day operations halt. The airline operated six flights to destinations including Mumbai, Beirut, and Cairo. The carrier has scheduled 48 flights through May 2 while using alternative corridors to ensure the movement of a larger number of passengers.

articles.headline: Indian IT firms step up acquisitions focusing on AI and cloud
articles.summary: Indian IT services companies have stepped up acquisitions, with over $4 billion in deals recorded in the second half of FY26. Firms like TCS, Infosys, and Wipro are acquiring specialized firms to build AI, cloud, and data engineering capabilities. Analysts suggest this strategy is aimed at meeting client demand for AI-led transformation rather than just expanding headcount.

articles.headline: Government cautiously optimistic on wheat output despite weather damage
articles.summary: The Agriculture Ministry remains cautiously optimistic about wheat production for 2025-26, pegging output at 120.21 million tonnes. While higher acreage and early sowing supported growth, unseasonal rains and heat stress in some regions caused localized damage. Procurement is currently lower than last year, with drops reported in Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Rajasthan.

articles.headline: PVR Inox stock shows bullish trend after double-bottom pattern
articles.summary: Technical analysis of PVR Inox stock suggests a bullish trend as the price rose above both 21- and 50-day moving averages last week. The stock has formed a strong base between ₹900 and ₹920, potentially creating a double-bottom chart pattern. Analysts recommend a buy at current levels with a stop-loss at ₹900 and a profit target of ₹1,250 as the rally continues.

articles.headline: New HOEC chief hints at strategic partnerships for expansion
articles.summary: Baroruchi Mishra, the new MD and CEO of Hindustan Oil Exploration Company, plans to forge strategic partnerships to unlock scale in technically complex deepwater areas. Immediate priorities include resolving technical issues at the B-80 oil field and tripling production at the Dirok gas field in Assam once a key pipeline interconnection is completed by the Indradhanush Gas Grid.

articles.headline: NSS survey shows spike in ailments and insurance coverage gaps
articles.summary: The National Sample Survey's 80th round shows the number of Indians reporting ailments rose to 13.1%, driven by a two-to-threefold jump in chronic issues like diabetes. While health insurance coverage expanded to over 44%, households still face high out-of-pocket expenses. Private hospitals now handle a larger share of childbirths despite costing seven times more than public facilities.

articles.headline: Analysis: Global economic disruption caused by superpower conflicts
articles.summary: Columnist TCA Srinivasa Raghavan argues that unwarranted attacks by superpowers like the US and Russia have caused massive global havoc. The ongoing West Asia conflict has triggered high energy costs and supply disruptions. The analysis warns of a decade of directionless economic activity, rising inflation, and massive unemployment due to the impairment of government intervention capacity.

articles.headline: Synergy of rooftop solar and school meals can drive e-cooking
articles.summary: Experts suggest pushing electric cooking in India by integrating it with government schemes like PM Surya Ghar and PM POSHAN. Currently, only 5% of Indian households use e-cooking. Convergence with housing and school feeding programs could help India achieve clean energy targets while providing a robust ecosystem for battery-supported solar hybrid systems in rural and urban areas.

articles.headline: Gen-Z voters influence family choices in Tamil Nadu polls
articles.summary: A shift in voting patterns was observed in recent Tamil Nadu polls, with youngsters from the newly formed Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam influencing the decisions of parents and grandparents. This 'Gen-Z era' trend saw many families voting for new symbols over traditional Dravidian parties, suggesting that youth-led political awareness is reshaping the state's electoral outcomes.

articles.headline: APTEL directs DERC to liquidate regulatory assets within three weeks
articles.summary: The Appellate Tribunal for Electricity has directed the Delhi Electricity Regulatory Commission to begin liquidating regulatory assets within three weeks. APTEL criticized DERC for delaying recovery, which increases the burden on consumers. Discoms in India are estimated to hold ₹3 lakh crore in regulatory assets, with half concentrated in Delhi, Tamil Nadu, Rajasthan, and Maharashtra.

articles.headline: CERC proposes market coupling to discover uniform electricity prices
articles.summary: The Central Electricity Regulatory Commission has introduced draft regulations for market coupling in power trading. The mechanism aims to link India's three power exchanges to discover a uniform market-clearing price. Government-owned Grid India is designated as the sole operator. While intended to boost competition, critics worry it may penalize exchanges that led market innovation.

articles.headline: North India faces intensified winter temperature spikes
articles.summary: A study shows that northern India has experienced a winter daytime cooling trend since 1980 due to aerosol forcing and heavy irrigation. However, this masking effect may soon vanish as clean air policies reduce aerosols and groundwater depletion limits irrigation. As these cooling factors change, winter warming in the northern plains could accelerate faster than the national average.

articles.headline: Analysis: Current AI investment wave mirrors early e-commerce growth
articles.summary: Karan Taurani of Elara Capital argues that the AI investment cycle resembles early e-commerce rather than the dot-com bubble. AI is being funded by cash-rich platform companies with large internal cash generation. Like e-commerce, AI may require five to seven years of upfront losses for habit formation before turning profitable, making it a 'cash-funded habit-creation cycle' for incumbents.

articles.headline: Perfetti Van Melle India targets ₹4,000 crore turnover
articles.summary: Confectionery major Perfetti Van Melle India aims to reach a ₹4,000 crore turnover this year by focusing on premium products priced at ₹5 and above. MD Nikhil Sharma stated that GST reforms have improved cost structures, allowing for more innovation in the jellies and gums segments. The company plans to grow the revenue share of its premium brands like Chupa Chups and Mentos to 50%.

articles.headline: Investors shift toward long-term planning on wealth-tech platforms
articles.summary: India's wealth-tech sector is evolving as retail investors shift from short-term trading to long-term goals like retirement. Funding in the segment reached $135.9 million in 2025, with capital flowing toward financial planning and advisory platforms. Platforms like Dezerv report near 100% retention for guided customers, signaling a move toward monetisation linked to assets under management.

articles.headline: Grahaa Space develops nano satellites for near real-time earth video
articles.summary: Bengaluru-based startup Grahaa Space is building a constellation of nano satellites designed to deliver near real-time high-definition video of the earth. The startup has patented proprietary optical payloads and data compression technologies to reduce latency in earth observation. Grahaa is preparing for orbital missions with private launch players and has signed 15 MOU for upcoming missions.

articles.headline: Piper Serica targets early-stage deep-tech and AI sectors
articles.summary: Venture capital firm Piper Serica is scaling its investment in early-stage Indian innovation with a focus on semiconductors, space-tech, and AI. Director Ajay Modi stated the firm offers initial cheques of ₹8–10 crore with a five-to-seven-year horizon. The firm has invested in 33 companies so far and is launching a second fund dedicated to growth-stage opportunities in industrial tech.

articles.headline: NaBFID targets 10% share in India’s infrastructure financing
articles.summary: NaBFID aims to grow its share of India’s infrastructure financing to 10% within five years. MD Rajkiran Rai G said the institution is using partial credit enhancement facilities to boost bond ratings and attract pension and insurance funds. NaBFID’s loan book reached ₹1.17 lakh crore in FY26, with a focus on roads, renewables, and urban infrastructure like water and healthcare.

articles.headline: Guaranteed income plans offer financial security for retirees
articles.summary: As life expectancy in India rises over 70, life insurers are promoting non-participating guaranteed income plans to ensure stable retirement funds. These annuity products provide fixed cash flows to protect against market volatility and inflation. Payouts can be linked to consumer price indices, helping retirees maintain their standard of living despite the absence of regular employment income.

articles.headline: New gold tokens offer 1% yield for investors
articles.summary: Singapore-based Mustafa Gold and FundBridge Capital have launched MG999 digital tokens that track gold prices and pay a 1% yield. The capital raised is lent to Mustafa Gold to purchase physical bullion for jewelry manufacturing. This synthetic upgrade of an ancient logic allows gold, traditionally a dead asset, to pay interest through a tokenization platform.

articles.headline: Bengaluru Airport corridor emerges as thriving business and residential hub
articles.summary: The region around Kempegowda International Airport is becoming a self-sustaining business hub as saturation in Whitefield and ORR pushes occupiers outward. Office leasing in the corridor accounts for 10% of the city's total, driven by aviation, logistics, and manufacturing firms. Rentals remain competitive at ₹60-100 per sq/ft, attracting significant interest from MNCs and NRI investors.

articles.headline: Dubai hotel chains offer temporary roles in India during slowdown
articles.summary: Premium hotel chains in Dubai are providing temporary assignments in India and Southeast Asia for employees as several properties close for renovation amid a regional business slowdown. Chains including Hyatt and Radisson are using flexible scheduling and internal mobility to manage workforces while occupancy levels in Dubai have dropped to 20-30% in recent weeks.

articles.headline: India's air cargo growth slows to 6.2% in FY26
articles.summary: Air cargo movement across Indian airports grew 6.2% to 39.63 lakh tonnes in FY26, slowing from the 10.5% growth seen in the previous year. Chennai emerged as the fastest-growing hub with 11.7% growth, while Delhi remained the largest. Experts attribute the slowdown to flight suspensions caused by the West Asia crisis, despite strong demand for electronics under the PLI scheme.

articles.headline: Future of 66,000 Paytm Payments Bank correspondents uncertain
articles.summary: The future of approximately 66,000 business correspondents and 719 staff members is in limbo after the RBI cancelled Paytm Payments Bank's licence on April 24. The cancellation raises concerns about the viability of the payments bank model. These correspondents provided banking access across 15,000 villages, and their potential displacement represents a challenge for financial inclusion.

articles.headline: How India’s AI policy panel affects startups
articles.summary: The government has established the AI Governance and Economic Group, an inter-ministerial advisory body to coordinate artificial intelligence policy. This primer breaks down how the new body will affect startups, addressing current ambiguities in laws like the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and IT Act, 2000, and comparing India's approach to the EU and US, without immediately creating binding law but laying groundwork for future regulations.

articles.headline: Lessons from Korea trade deal for India
articles.summary: India and South Korea are reviewing their 16-year-old Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) to balance trade, as South Korea has reaped greater gains, with India's trade deficit tripling. India's exports to Korea shifted from labour-intensive sectors to capital goods, while imports deepened India's dependency on Korean industrial inputs. India maintains a services surplus, driven by computer services, indicating areas for better negotiation in future FTAs.

articles.headline: Big IT concedes AI shadow on growth
articles.summary: Major Indian IT firms like TCS, Infosys, HCLTech, Wipro, and Tech Mahindra acknowledge that artificial intelligence (AI) is eroding revenue and lowering billing rates due to increased productivity. While companies expect increased tech work to offset this, analysts are not convinced AI-driven revenue will be meaningfully additive to near-term growth, signaling a turbulent FY27 for the industry amid client-specific and geopolitical concerns.

articles.headline: India plans city-level data on jobs, enterprises to aid policy
articles.summary: The National Statistics Office (NSO) plans to develop annual statistical reports for 47 Indian cities with over one million population, covering labour force participation and enterprise status. This initiative aims to bridge critical data gaps, provide an evidence base for urban policy and planning, and support informed decision-making at all governance levels, reflecting India's rapid urbanization and economic growth.

articles.headline: India plans to boost infra for firefighting
articles.summary: India's government is overhauling its firefighting vehicle infrastructure, from identifying urban challenges to building domestic testing capacity. This effort seeks to address critical gaps in fire safety amid acute shortages of fire stations, equipment, and personnel, raising concerns over response readiness as urban fire risks escalate. Consultations with vehicle makers are underway to equip testing centres like Arai.

articles.headline: PM hails clean power push, digital census
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi highlighted India's progress in clean energy and the upcoming digital census in his 'Mann Ki Baat' address. He noted the Kalpakkam fast breeder reactor achieved criticality, India added 6GW wind capacity taking total to 56GW, and overall renewable energy reached 283.46GW. Modi also emphasized the 2027 census as the first fully digital exercise, with enumerators using mobile apps and citizens verifying data.

articles.headline: STT hike takes a steep toll on F&O volumes in April
articles.summary: The hike in securities transaction tax (STT) from 1 April and uncertainty from the West Asia war have significantly impacted index futures and options (F&O) volumes. Average daily turnover for Nifty and Bank Nifty futures plunged 33% in April from March. Analysts state the STT hike increased breakeven points for traders, leading to lower profits and wider bid-ask spreads, while the war added to market volatility.

articles.headline: Why Shriram’s FY27 path is rocky
articles.summary: Shriram Finance reported steady Q4FY26 results with 15% AUM growth to ₹3 trillion, driven by commercial and passenger vehicles. However, macro concerns like elevated crude prices, weak freight demand, and potential dull monsoon pose risks to future growth and asset quality. The company aims for 17-18% AUM growth in FY27, shifting towards new-vehicle financing and non-vehicle segments like gold loans and MSME lending.

articles.headline: Most of the worst is already priced in, says Old Bridge MF’s Andrade
articles.summary: Kenneth Andrade, CIO of Old Bridge Mutual Fund, believes most negative market factors are priced in, making now a good window to gradually build portfolios. He expects modest returns in the low teens over the next couple of years, advising deploying capital staggered. He highlights opportunities in smaller industries undergoing consolidation and suggests selling when valuations become exceptionally tight or new players enter mature markets.

articles.headline: Fintech pivots to B2B, infra
articles.summary: Early-stage fintech investors in India are shifting from consumer-facing models to B2B software and infrastructure businesses. This pivot is driven by tighter regulations on digital lending and data governance, which increased compliance costs. Funds now prioritize asset-light, recurring-revenue models serving financial institutions, seeking steady growth with minimal policy risk, reflecting a broader trend of banks upgrading their technology stacks.

articles.headline: CP Milk in early talks to sell up to 15% at ₹3,000 cr valuation
articles.summary: Promoters of Lucknow-based CP Milk and Food Products are in early talks to sell a 10-15% stake to private equity investors, potentially valuing the company at ₹2,500-3,000 crore. This marks the company's first external capital raise, aiming for ₹300-400 crore to expand geographical and product footprint. CP Milk, the largest private dairy in eastern Uttar Pradesh, reported ₹1,773.35 crore operating income in FY25.

articles.headline: Startups lean on agility as giants eye microdrama space
articles.summary: The microdrama segment faces disruption as major players like JioHotstar and Amazon MX Player enter, raising production and talent standards. Startups are pivoting to lean, high-quality series amid consolidation in a crowded market of over 40 players. Legacy platforms have distribution advantages, but microdramas reward speed and fast-paced stories designed for mobile-first, vertical-screen experiences, favoring agile new entrants.

articles.headline: Persistent plans large European acquisition
articles.summary: Mid-sized IT firm Persistent Systems is targeting a 'sizeable acquisition' in Europe to increase the continent’s share in overall revenues to 20% from 8%, aiming to diversify beyond the US market. The company is scouting for targets that align with its value systems and widen its capabilities, with cash and investments of ₹2,762 crore as of 31 March available for this expansion.

articles.headline: Pvt banks see asset quality rise, geopolitical risks linger
articles.summary: India’s top private-sector banks showed improved asset quality in the March quarter with stable or declining GNPA ratios, despite no visible stress from the Iran war. Lenders, however, maintain a cautious undertone, increasing buffers and stress-testing portfolios against adverse global scenarios, with Axis Bank notably frontloading ₹2,001 crore in precautionary provisions due to unpredictable macro and geopolitical uncertainties.

articles.headline: Tweak in NPS commissions may fail to lure distributors
articles.summary: A recent change in the commission structure for onboarding clients to the National Pension System (NPS) may not significantly attract distributors (Points of Presence or PoPs), as earnings remain lower than those offered by mutual funds. PoPs can now earn 0.20% of assets under management per annum as trail commission, plus a ₹200 onboarding fee, but mutual funds offer higher commissions up to 2.1% of AUM.

articles.headline: AI to affect salary growth in 2-3 years: TeamLease
articles.summary: TeamLease Edtech predicts employees using AI will gain an edge in increments over the next 2-3 years, especially in sectors like technology, GCCs, and BFSI, as AI skills become core to workforce strategy. Surveys indicate 92% of Indian knowledge workers use AI, and 80% of leaders prefer less experienced candidates with AI skills. This shift will lead to AI-influenced appraisal frameworks and faster progression in digitally intensive roles.

articles.headline: Fed set to lead uneasy G-7 with rates kept on hold this week
articles.summary: G-7 policymakers are expected to maintain steady interest rates this week, closely monitoring signs of higher energy costs potentially fanning inflation, especially due to the Iran war. The Bank of Japan, Bank of Canada, Bank of England, and European Central Bank are all anticipated to echo cautious 'wait and watch' messages. The US Justice Department ending a Fed building investigation could clear a path for Kevin Warsh as Fed chair.

articles.headline: Musk nears financial services rollout on X
articles.summary: Elon Musk is close to launching 'X Money,' a banking and payments platform within X (formerly Twitter), aimed at creating an 'everything app' similar to China's WeChat. Early users report competitive perks like 3% cashback and 6% interest on savings. Musk, a PayPal co-founder, views payments as crucial for his super app vision, allowing users to live their lives on the X platform.

articles.headline: India is vital to Google’s global AI strategy, says Jeff Dean
articles.summary: Google's global chief scientist Jeff Dean stated India is vital to the company's global AI strategy, with its engineering hubs developing technologies specifically for the Indian market that are then exported worldwide. He cited a global flood prediction model, 'long context window' for Gemini, and AI models for agriculture as key Indian contributions, emphasizing the collaboration with government agencies and local experts.

articles.headline: The race to make the world’s most in-demand machine
articles.summary: ASML, the world’s sole supplier of machines for making cutting-edge chips at scale, is racing to meet surging demand driven by hundreds of billions of dollars in AI infrastructure investments from tech giants. The Dutch company plans to increase production significantly, investing in new facilities and engineers, while working on more advanced machines, to avoid being a bottleneck for its customers in the global AI race.

articles.headline: Wall Street is sorting software firms into winners and losers
articles.summary: Wall Street is seeing varied confidence in software companies amidst fears of AI disruption, with loans from vertical software and cybersecurity firms performing better than those from horizontal software and software-engineering companies. Investors prioritize software that controls workflow or handles sensitive data, like tax compliance and human-capital management tools, over commodified tasks like data analysis, indicating a shift towards 'defensive moats'.

articles.headline: Strikes highlight nuclear risks of Russia-Ukraine war
articles.summary: Strikes across Ukraine, Russian-occupied territory, and Russia killed at least 16 people, prompting fresh warnings about nuclear risks. Ukraine's President Zelenskyy said Russia's attacks risked repeating history on the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl disaster. The strikes included a drone attack on Russia’s Yaroslavl oil refinery, sparking fires and impacting Russian military fuel supplies, and missile strikes in Dnipro and Luhansk.

articles.headline: A British king tries to stop another historic break with America
articles.summary: King Charles III's US visit aims to mend strained UK-US ties, currently at their lowest point since the 1950s, ahead of America's Declaration of Independence anniversary. President Trump has criticized PM Keir Starmer, threatening to reopen trade deals and questioning alliances. The visit, following a security incident at a Washington hotel, presents an awkward backdrop, though Trump expressed hope it could repair relations.

articles.headline: India-NZ outreach on FTA signing eve
articles.summary: India's commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal and New Zealand's minister Todd McClay met industry leaders in Agra to highlight opportunities ahead of signing a free trade agreement (FTA). The pact aims to boost trade, investment, talent mobility, and collaboration across sectors like market access and agriculture, benefiting manufacturers, farmers, MSMEs, women entrepreneurs, students, and skilled professionals.

articles.headline: India plugs oil supply gap as West Asia supplies sink
articles.summary: India significantly increased purchases of Russian oil and revived alternate supplies from Africa, Iran, and Venezuela to mitigate crude shortfalls from the crisis-ridden West Asia. India, the world's third-largest oil buyer, plugged supply gaps by nearly doubling Russian imports to 1.98 million bpd in March, aided by a temporary US waiver, and received crude from Angola (327,000 bpd), Iran (276,000 bpd), and Venezuela (137,000 bpd).

articles.headline: Adani Green to add 10GWh storage
articles.summary: Adani Green Energy Ltd plans to invest approximately ₹15,000 crore to add over 10GWh of battery energy storage capacity in the current financial year. This expansion, occurring at its Khavda renewable energy park in Gujarat, aims to deliver reliable, dispatchable clean power, especially during peak evening demand, and support round-the-clock renewable energy generation amidst India's accelerating energy transition.

articles.headline: CIL plans roadmap to cut coal imports
articles.summary: State-owned Coal India Ltd (CIL) is developing a 10-year roadmap to significantly reduce India's 243 million tonnes of coal imports. The plan includes boosting domestic production, upgrading coal quality, improving logistics, and conducting a forensic audit of imports. This initiative aligns with India's goals for energy security, lower foreign exchange outflows, and green transition targets under the national coal gasification mission.

articles.headline: Diversified strategy to boost China exports, cut imports
articles.summary: India is adopting a diversified strategy to boost exports to China while simultaneously reducing import dependence, aiming to strengthen domestic capacities. A senior official stated that 'decoupling from Beijing is difficult' as Chinese inputs are crucial for India's industrial growth, particularly raw materials, intermediate, capital goods, and active pharmaceutical ingredients, which support domestic manufacturing and exports.

articles.headline: Goyal to meet exporters, industry associations today
articles.summary: Commerce and industry minister Piyush Goyal will meet with export promotion councils and industry associations in New Delhi to discuss strategies for boosting India’s outbound shipments. This meeting follows a significant 7.44% decline in merchandise exports in March, impacted by the West Asia crisis and global trade uncertainties, with shipments to West Asian countries contracting by over 50%.

articles.headline: COAI seeks lower spectrum prices
articles.summary: The Cellular Operators Association of India (COAI) has urged the government to lower spectrum bid prices for the upcoming auction. COAI argues that service providers have not yet recovered substantial investments in 5G infrastructure, leading to a low return on investment (ROI). They emphasize that spectrum cost is the biggest capital expenditure and requires government support until ROI is achieved.

articles.headline: Wrap wage revision in a year, PSBs told
articles.summary: The government has instructed public sector banks (PSBs) to initiate and finalize negotiations for the 13th Bipartite Settlement within 12 months. This directive aims to ensure a timely wage revision for employees and officers, which is due from 1 November 2027, and maintain industrial harmony. PSBs and other financial institutions revise employee wages every five years.

articles.headline: Marriott bullish on India expansion
articles.summary: Hospitality chain Marriott International remains confident about its expansion in India, with a pipeline of 200 hotels and an aggressive opening strategy. Despite acknowledging short-term disruptions to global travel flows due to the West Asia crisis, particularly affecting transit through the Middle East, Marriott emphasizes its long-term commitment to the Indian market and its substantial growth potential.

articles.headline: FlixBus sees India as its top market
articles.summary: Global travel-tech firm FlixBus expects India to become its largest market by passenger volume by 2030, highlighting its strategic importance. Having entered India in February 2024 with intercity bus services, the Munich-headquartered company plans to scale operations, citing India as the world's second-largest bus market. The 'air-conditioned intercity' segment alone is larger than the European and US markets combined.

articles.headline: Mamata faces ‘poriborton’ challenge in West Bengal election
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee faces a 'poriborton' (change) challenge in the upcoming state elections, seeking a fourth term. Key concerns among voters include lack of development, widespread unemployment, grassroots corruption ('cut money'), crumbling law and order (e.g., Sandeshkhali), and a perceived perpetuation of 'Left culture'. The BJP aims to capitalize on anti-incumbency, while Banerjee relies on welfarism and her strong image, particularly among minorities.

articles.headline: Market volatility drags SIP returns—but time does the heavy lifting
articles.summary: One-year SIP returns across equity mutual funds turned negative in March 2026 due to market volatility stemming from the West Asia war, leading to a spike in SIP stoppage ratios to 100%. While newer investors show nervousness, experts advise long-term investors to continue SIPs, emphasizing that market corrections allow for rupee cost averaging—buying more units at lower NAVs—which amplifies gains when markets recover.

articles.headline: Porting? Don’t lose waiting period credits
articles.summary: You can port your health insurance policy to a new insurer to retain waiting period benefits, including for pre-existing conditions, under Irdai regulations. Apply at least 45 days before renewal; the sum assured cannot exceed the existing one for full waiver. For life insurance, existing policies usually don't allow sum assured increases; buying a second policy is recommended to bridge coverage gaps, factoring in income and outstanding loans.

articles.headline: ‘AI can do a lot. But so can humans’
articles.summary: Aneesh Raman, LinkedIn EVP and Chief Economic Opportunity Officer, discusses AI's impact on work, advocating a skills-first hiring approach over traditional degrees. He suggests workers focus on human 'soft skills' and adopt a 'builder mentality,' using AI tools to up-level their work. Raman believes AI will expand opportunities for entrepreneurial-minded individuals, emphasizing continuous learning and collaboration to navigate evolving job roles.

articles.headline: Lobby the boss to send you on that business trip
articles.summary: Despite declining corporate travel due to rising costs, travelling for work offers crucial professional opportunities and career advantages. While boomers and Gen Xers are less enthusiastic, 86% of millennials believe business trips open professional doors. Junior workers, often left out when travel budgets tighten, are encouraged to lobby for trips, as being 'in the room where it happens' facilitates deals and unexpected opportunities.

articles.headline: Trump in crosshairs again as shooter targets DC function
articles.summary: A 31-year-old gunman, Cole Tomas Allen, fired a shotgun at a Secret Service agent guarding the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, targeting President Donald Trump and other officials. Trump, first lady Melania Trump, and Vice President JD Vance were safely evacuated. The suspect, identified as a "Friendly Federal Assassin," had grievances over Trump administration policies.

articles.headline: Bengal battle reaches fever pitch in final lap
articles.summary: Political leaders including PM Modi, CM Mamata Banerjee, and HM Amit Shah campaigned intensely across West Bengal for the second phase of assembly elections. Modi focused on women's safety and CAA, while Banerjee accused central forces of EVM interference. The campaign for the April 29 polls concludes Monday evening.

articles.headline: ED raids in Kolkata in fraud cases linked to fugitive
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted searches at multiple locations in Kolkata in connection with alleged land grabbing and financial fraud cases. The raids are linked to fugitive criminal 'Sona Pappu', a senior official confirmed. Residences of two businessmen in Anandapur and Alipore areas were targeted.

articles.headline: Delhi continues to swelter, IMD forecasts some respite from heat
articles.summary: Delhi experienced a maximum temperature of 42.1°C on Sunday. The city is likely to experience similar conditions on Monday with a "yellow alert" for heat. Spells of rain may bring some relief from Tuesday onwards, according to IMD.

articles.headline: Delhi cop shoots dead delivery executive after noise dispute
articles.summary: A Delhi Police head constable was arrested after he allegedly shot dead a 21-year-old delivery executive and injured another man in Dwarka. The incident occurred in the early hours of Sunday following an altercation over noise.

articles.headline: Raghu Rai, icon whose lens chronicled India, passes away
articles.summary: Raghu Rai, a defining photographer of South Asia, died in New Delhi at 83 after battling cancer. Known for photojournalism, he chronicled India’s evolving political and social conscience for over five decades, capturing events from Bangladesh to Bhopal and shaping India Today's formative years.

articles.headline: AAP split puts ‘merger’ clause of defection law under scrutiny
articles.summary: The defection of seven Rajya Sabha MPs from AAP to BJP triggers a constitutional question on the Tenth Schedule's merger clause. Rebels claim protection under Paragraph 4, requiring two-thirds legislative support, but legal interpretation is unsettled, potentially needing Supreme Court clarity on party-level versus legislative mergers.

articles.headline: Social burden of Bengal’s regional exceptionalism
articles.summary: This two-part series examines West Bengal's regional exceptionalism, focusing on its "social contract." The first part highlights a social regression embedded in the state’s politics, noting its unique demography with large Dalit and Muslim populations but disproportionate upper-caste Hindu dominance in political leadership.

articles.headline: SC flags growing menace of drugs in campuses
articles.summary: The Supreme Court expressed concern over drug networks infiltrating educational institutions, systematically targeting students as consumers and conduits. It paused action against a 21-year-old law student from Hyderabad caught with ganja, noting the disproportionate impact of prosecution on young lives and suggesting an amicus curiae.

articles.headline: Karnataka SC quota decision draws BJP’s ire, Cong backs it
articles.summary: Karnataka's government decided to introduce revised internal reservation within Scheduled Castes (SC), drawing criticism from the BJP and support from the Congress. The quota divides the 15% SC reservation into 5.25% for 'right-hand' and 'left-hand' Dalits, and 4.5% for other SC groups.

articles.headline: DKS visits ailing minister Sudhakar at KIMS Hospital
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy Chief Minister DK Shivakumar visited Planning and Statistics Minister D Sudhakar, who is critically ill with a lung infection at KIMS Hospital. Sudhakar is on full life support, and lung transplantation is being considered. CM Siddaramaiah also visited him earlier.

articles.headline: Bengaluru police arrest six from Tamil Nadu in dacoity case
articles.summary: Bengaluru police arrested six individuals from Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, in connection with a dacoity case. The accused allegedly trespassed into a house, assaulted occupants, and robbed cash, phones, laptops, and a two-wheeler. Police recovered stolen items worth ₹3 lakh.

articles.headline: Two held for two-wheeler thefts in Bengaluru, 20 vehicles seized
articles.summary: Bengaluru police arrested two individuals for two-wheeler thefts, recovering 20 stolen vehicles worth ₹15 lakhs. The arrests followed a complaint, and the accused confessed to their involvement in multiple thefts across the city.

articles.headline: Plan to revive campus polls invites enthusiasm, unease
articles.summary: Karnataka government's move to restore student union elections has mixed reactions. Ministers and student leaders agreed on the return of polls, but concerns remain about political influence and past campus unrest that led to the system's dismantling over three decades ago due to violence.

articles.headline: Liquor industry flags excise plan’s uneven impact
articles.summary: Karnataka's proposed Alcohol-in-Beverage (AIB) taxation system faces mixed industry reactions. While seen as a structural shift, bodies like CIABC caution it risks disproportionately affecting high-volume, lower-priced Indian Made Foreign Liquor (IMFL) segments, potentially impacting revenue and consumption patterns unevenly across categories.

articles.headline: Bullet fired at woman lawyer’s Belagavi house
articles.summary: A bullet was fired at the residence of advocate Pooja Sanjay Kakatkar in Belagavi district early Sunday, striking a first-floor window. No injuries were reported, but the incident occurred minutes after her daughter moved downstairs. Police are investigating potential motives and have registered a case.

articles.headline: Snakebite toll rises to 7 in Kerala as 2 more die
articles.summary: Two elderly women died from snakebites in Kannur and Idukki, Kerala, raising the state's snakebite death toll to seven. With soaring temperatures, experts warn snakes may venture into houses. The CM's Office announced state support for a 10-year-old undergoing treatment.

articles.headline: Yellow alert issued in Kerala for high temperature
articles.summary: The IMD issued a yellow alert for high temperatures in Kerala for two days, forecasting maximums up to 40°C in Palakkad and 39°C in Kollam. Temperatures are expected to be 3-4°C above normal, with hot and humid weather prevailing in most affected districts.

articles.headline: Azharuddin, Kodandaram nominated as Telangana MLCs
articles.summary: Telangana Governor Shiv Pratap Shukla nominated former cricketer Mohammad Azharuddin and retired professor M Kodandaram as MLCs under the governor’s quota. Azharuddin's nomination follows a delay, as he needed to be elected to the legislature within six months of being sworn in as minister.

articles.headline: Telangana CM assures extending Prajavani services to grassroots
articles.summary: Telangana CM A Revanth Reddy directed officials to expand Prajavani services to the grassroots level, ensuring priority resolution of public petitions. Prajavani, launched in December 2023, aims to address grievances expeditiously, with plans for a unified digital platform and dedicated nodal officers.

articles.headline: Thrissur firecracker blast toll rises to 16
articles.summary: One more person hospitalized after the Mundathikode firecracker explosion died, raising the death toll to 16. The deceased was identified as Unnikrishnan, one of 38 people present at the unit manufacturing fireworks for Thrissur Pooram festivities. Four others remain missing, and two are critical.

articles.headline: Self-enumeration for Census 2027 launched in Telangana
articles.summary: The self-enumeration phase for Census 2027 commenced in Telangana, inaugurated by Governor Shiv Pratap Shukla. This marks India’s first fully digital census. The phase runs from April 26 to May 10, followed by house-listing from May 11 to June 9. CM Revanth Reddy also participated.

articles.headline: Kerala churches forgo fireworks in annual festivals
articles.summary: Several Malankara Orthodox churches in Kerala decided to forgo fireworks during their annual feasts. This decision follows an appeal by Baselios Marthoma Mathews III after the Mundathikode firecracker explosion killed 15 people, with funds now diverted towards charitable activities.

articles.headline: Andhra govt eyes apple cultivation after Anantapur success
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh government is considering scaling up apple cultivation after a farmer's unexpected success in drought-prone Anantapur district. The KLD apple variety yielded high prices (₹170/kg) and promises faster yields than traditional northern Indian apples, offering diversification for farmers.

articles.headline: Women and Matuas suffered under Mamata regime: Modi
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi intensified his attack on TMC, alleging women and the Dalit Matua community suffered during Mamata Banerjee’s 15-year tenure in West Bengal. He promised free rice, clean water, solar plants, and Mudra Loans, reiterating citizenship under CAA for refugees and vowing to drive out infiltrators.

articles.headline: Central forces discuss security ahead of Bengal ph-2 polls
articles.summary: Heads of Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) met to discuss security for West Bengal's second phase of assembly elections on April 29. Over 250,000 personnel are deployed to ensure a peaceful and fair democratic process, with "Operation Night Dominance" reinforcing security in sensitive areas.

articles.headline: 100 crude bombs recovered from ‘TMC worker’ house in Bengal
articles.summary: Around 100 crude bombs were reportedly recovered from the residence of an alleged TMC worker in Bhangar, South Paraganas district, West Bengal. This incident occurred days before the second phase of assembly polls. Police acted on specific inputs, with the EC official warning of action against officers for laxity.

articles.headline: TVK candidates told to inspect strong rooms for EVMs
articles.summary: TVK leader Aadhav Arjuna said his party chief Vijay instructed all contesting candidates in the Tamil Nadu April 23 assembly elections to inspect strong rooms on a rotation basis. This is to ensure the safety and security of Electronic Voting Machines (EVMs).

articles.headline: Kejriwal asks deleted voters to support TMC, defeat BJP
articles.summary: AAP leader Arvind Kejriwal urged voters whose names were deleted from West Bengal's electoral rolls to support TMC and help Mamata Banerjee defeat BJP. He alleged a BJP-ECI conspiracy led to 9 million deletions during SIR, aiming to target citizenship documents, a claim Mamata alone can fight.

articles.headline: TMC’s reign of terror prevails: Rahul on Cong worker’s death
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi condemned the alleged murder of party worker Debdeep Chatterjee by TMC workers in Asansol post-poll violence. He stated "TMC’s reign of terror" prevails in West Bengal, contrasting it with India's non-violent tradition. TMC's Kunal Ghosh alleged Gandhi was "working for the BJP."

articles.headline: BJP poisoning communal harmony: Hemant Soren
articles.summary: Jharkhand CM Hemant Soren accused BJP of "poisoning communal harmony" in India, urging West Bengal voters to defeat them in assembly polls. Speaking at a roadshow for TMC, he alleged BJP's divisive politics rupture social fabric and called for supporting TMC to safeguard secular values.

articles.headline: Gujarat local polls: Low turnout in municipalities, high in rural
articles.summary: Polling for Gujarat’s local body elections concluded, with turnout below 50% in municipal corporations (48.55%) but above 60% in district (60.22%) and taluka (61.08%) panchayats. The elections, held amid a heatwave, are the first since implementing 27% OBC reservation. BJP won over 700 seats uncontested.

articles.headline: AI, automation: India’s middle class at breaking point
articles.summary: A new book, "Break Point," argues India's middle class faces a crisis due to AI and automation, wage stagnation, and rising debt. It claims poor higher education quality leads to a graduate employability crisis and increased household debt, exacerbated by social media's "industrialisation of aspirations."

articles.headline: Centre, Ladakhi groups to resume talks on May 22: Lt Guv
articles.summary: The Centre will resume talks with Ladakh representatives on May 22 to discuss demands for statehood and inclusion under the Sixth Schedule. Lieutenant Governor Vinai Kumar Saxena announced the meeting of the sub-committee for political dialogue, aiming for an "enduring solution" to meet aspirations.

articles.headline: Woman killed in leopard attack in UP
articles.summary: A 32-year-old woman, Sandhya Devi, was mauled to death by a leopard while sleeping outside her hut in Lakhimpur Kheri, UP, within the Dudhwa buffer zone. The attack triggered protests from residents demanding the leopard's capture. Forest teams have been deployed to monitor and capture the animal.

articles.headline: AAP submits disqualification plea against 7 MPs to RS chair
articles.summary: The AAP submitted a petition to the Rajya Sabha Chairman seeking disqualification of seven MPs who quit the party and announced a "merger" with BJP. AAP leader Sanjay Singh cited the Tenth Schedule's defection provisions, while former Lok Sabha secretary general PDT Achary said the final decision rests with the Chairman.

articles.headline: AAP supporters detained after protest outside Chadha’s house
articles.summary: Mumbai police detained AAP activists protesting outside Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha’s residence in Khar. AAP's Mumbai unit president Preeti Sharma Menon claimed police prevented peaceful protest against Chadha, who defected to BJP. Police confirmed detentions without elaborating.

articles.headline: NCB launches Operation WIPE against online drug trade
articles.summary: The Narcotics Control Bureau (NCB) launched "Operation WIPE" nationwide to combat online drug trafficking. This proactive shift monitors illicit activities on digital platforms using advanced tools and international intelligence. So far, NCB flagged 122 instances involving 62 substances, with online platforms suspending vendors.

articles.headline: NIT Kurukshetra director resigns, senior professor takes charge
articles.summary: BV Raman Reddy, NIT Kurukshetra director, resigned on April 4, accepted on April 24, following student suicides and the withdrawal of his administrative powers. Senior Professor Brahmjit Singh has been appointed officiating Director. Reddy's tenure was stripped of powers after an enquiry.

articles.headline: Raise awareness on summer heat action plans
articles.summary: The dreaded heatwave has begun, and while Heat Action Plans (HAPs) are in place, they often lack nuance for vulnerable communities. The article stresses the need for targeted planning, ensuring primary health centres are ready for heat stress, and public campaigns for awareness. Data collection should improve for future HAPs.

articles.headline: Modi urges citizens to actively participate in Census exercise
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi, during his 133rd 'Mann Ki Baat,' urged citizens to actively participate in Census 2027, calling it a "shared responsibility." He highlighted the digital self-enumeration option and discussed progress in nuclear/renewable energy (Fast Breeder Reactor achieved criticality, 56GW wind power capacity) and wildlife conservation (blackbucks reappeared in Chhattisgarh).

articles.headline: Air India flight makes priority landing after snag in Bhopal
articles.summary: A Bhopal-bound Air India flight from Mumbai, carrying 175 people, made a "priority" landing in Bhopal due to a technical snag. All safety protocols were activated, and the aircraft landed safely. The return leg to Mumbai was cancelled for inspection and rectification.

articles.headline: SC pushes software-led reset of remission system, pilot in 2 UP prisons
articles.summary: The Supreme Court mandated a technology-led intervention, an "E-Prisons Early Release Processing Module," to streamline remission applications. This digital system, developed by NIC, will be piloted in Lucknow and Agra jails to automatically identify eligible prisoners, reducing delays from the current paper-based process and enhancing transparency and accountability.

articles.headline: President Murmu to visit Shimla today on summer sojourn
articles.summary: President Droupadi Murmu will undertake a five-day summer sojourn to Himachal Pradesh starting Monday, staying at Rashtrapati Niwas, Mashobra, Shimla. She will visit the Army Training Command, Shimla, before returning to Delhi on May 1.

articles.headline: Bihar govt doctors oppose ban on private practice
articles.summary: The Bihar Health Services Association (BHSA) opposed the state government's proposed blanket ban on private practice by government doctors. BHSA warned that such a decision, outlined in an April 11 resolution, would not be accepted without prior consultation with the association.

articles.headline: Pocso court awards death to man for raping minor relative
articles.summary: A special Pocso court in Arunachal Pradesh's Yupia sentenced Lakang Tallang, 44, to death for repeatedly raping his minor relative in 2019. The court called it a "rarest of rare" case with a grave breach of trust, noting the convict was a repeat offender with no remorse.

articles.headline: Brics meeting fails consensus over West Asia crisis
articles.summary: A Brics meeting of senior officials failed to achieve consensus on the West Asia conflict due to sharp differences between Iran and UAE, who are party to the conflict. Discussions covered Palestine, Gaza, humanitarian aid, and terrorism, but efforts to bridge gaps were unsuccessful, leading to a chair's statement instead of a consensus document.

articles.headline: AIIMS New Delhi chief named NITI Aayog member
articles.summary: Dr. M Srinivas, Director of AIIMS New Delhi, has been appointed as a member of the NITI Aayog, India's premier policy think tank. Srinivas, who took charge of AIIMS in September 2022, still had time left in his tenure. AIIMS will likely get an interim director soon.

articles.headline: Vessel hit by projectiles, 3 crew members hurt
articles.summary: A Togo-flagged oil/chemical tanker, MT Chiron 7, was hit by projectiles or shrapnel in the West Asia war zone, injuring three crew members. No fatalities were reported, and the vessel sustained damage to its navigation bridge. The incident occurred amidst rising maritime risks in the region.

articles.headline: ‘Godman’ Kharat sent to judicial custody till May 9
articles.summary: Self-styled astrologer-cum-godman Ashok Kharat was sent to judicial custody till May 9 by a Nashik court. Kharat, arrested on March 18, faces a sixth case of sexual exploitation and large-scale financial fraud, accused of threatening women with "divine powers" to exploit them.

articles.headline: How India must deal with Trumpian insults
articles.summary: Happymon Jacob argues India should stop ignoring Trumpian disrespect, citing his "hellhole" insult. He suggests abandoning "chemistry experiments" for institutional outreach, publicly calling out name-calling, and refusing Trump's framing of favors. India, as a significant power, should assert its interests politically and diplomatically.

articles.headline: President of a divided America
articles.summary: The assassination attempt on US President Donald Trump highlights a larger social and political crisis in a polarized America. MAGA politics has normalized hate, and Trump's backtracking on promises has divided his base. The article suggests the onus is on the President to address the nation's divisions for peace.

articles.headline: UK PM condemns attacks on democratic institutions, press freedom
articles.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned any attack on democratic institutions or press freedom "in the strongest possible terms." This statement comes in the context of the shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner.

articles.headline: Delhi’s prescription for newborn testing is flawed
articles.summary: Chandrakant Lahariya criticizes Delhi's ANMOL initiative for proposing 56 blood tests on every newborn. He argues that screening for rare disorders without assured treatment is problematic, creates false positives, and burdens families and the health system. A more rational, evidence-based, and targeted approach is advocated.

articles.headline: Rights of citizens and etiquette of public protests
articles.summary: Shashi Shekhar discusses the impact of public protests and VIP movements on common citizens, citing a Mumbai woman's defiance against traffic jams caused by a BJP protest. He highlights the health, work, and environmental costs of disruptions, urging for designated protest spaces and policy changes to prevent traffic issues.

articles.headline: Gunfire, chaos on Trump’s night with the press
articles.summary: The White House Correspondents’ Association dinner was thrown into chaos by gunfire. A 31-year-old gunman, Cole Allen, staying at the hotel, fired rounds from a shotgun, injuring a Secret Service agent. Trump and first lady Melania Trump were rushed out, and the event was canceled, raising security questions.

articles.headline: Trump calls for unity, bipartisan healing
articles.summary: After the assassination attempt, Donald Trump called for unity and bipartisan healing, a shift from his usual combative style. He suggested his politics made him a target but was impressed by the "love and coming together" at the dinner, emphasizing the presidency as a "dangerous profession."

articles.headline: World leaders condemn Trump shooting incident
articles.summary: Global leaders, including PM Narendra Modi, Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu, Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi, French President Emmanuel Macron, and Italian PM Giorgia Meloni, condemned the attempted assassination on Donald Trump. They expressed relief that he was safe and emphasized that violence has no place in democracy.

articles.headline: Hindu man’s body found hanging from tree in Bangladesh
articles.summary: The body of Nayan Sadhu, 40, a Hindu temple caretaker, was found hanging from a tree in southeastern Bangladesh three days after he went missing. The Bangladesh Hindu Buddhist Christian Unity Council condemned the incident, demanding stern action against perpetrators. Police are investigating whether it was murder or suicide.

articles.headline: Iran caused billions in damage to US bases in West Asia: Report
articles.summary: A report by NBC News claims Iran caused billions of dollars in damage to US military bases across seven West Asia countries, far exceeding public acknowledgment. Damage spanned at least 11 bases, potentially costing $5 billion to repair, including an F-5 fighter jet bombing Camp Buehring in Kuwait.

articles.headline: Two former Israel PMs unite to challenge Bibi in elections
articles.summary: Former Israeli prime ministers Naftali Bennett and Yair Lapid announced they will run on a joint list, "Beyahad (Together)," in this year’s elections. The move aims to unseat incumbent Benjamin Netanyahu, whom both have criticized for his handling of the country’s wars since the October 7, 2023 Hamas attack.

articles.headline: Junior Shooting World Cup: India wins two medals
articles.summary: India won two more medals at the ISSF Junior Shooting World Cup in Cairo, with Hemant Burman taking silver in men’s 50m rifle 3 positions and Zuhair Khan/Addya Katyal winning bronze in trap mixed team. India finished top of the table with 16 medals.

articles.headline: Eshan Malinga: Silent architect in SRH’s revival
articles.summary: Eshan Malinga has been a silent architect in Sunrisers Hyderabad’s (SRH) revival in the IPL, sharing the top spot for most wickets (14) despite flying under the radar. His diverse skills, including "old-school wrists" for tricky ball movement and precise execution of yorkers and slower balls, have been crucial for SRH.

articles.headline: Badminton could lose essence with new 3x15 scoring: Coaches
articles.summary: Top Indian coaches, including U Vimal Kumar, warn that badminton's new 3x15 scoring format could diminish the sport's essence. They argue shorter matches (reduced by ~30 mins) would decrease tactical depth, physical endurance, and comeback chances, making the game less challenging and potentially less exciting for spectators.

articles.headline: Srinagar Khel Sankalp to strengthen sports culture
articles.summary: Sports Minister Mansukh Mandaviya released the Srinagar Khel Sankalp document, outlining a national vision to strengthen sports culture through "cooperative federalism." It focuses on athlete-centric development, infrastructure expansion, and boosting tourism, aiming to make India a sporting nation capable of hosting major global events.

articles.headline: Trump safe after shooting in Washington; man held
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump was uninjured after an armed man stormed the White House Correspondents’ Dinner lobby, firing shots. A Secret Service officer was shot in a bullet-resistant vest but is recovering. The suspect, Cole Tomas Allen (31), was detained. Trump believed he was the target. World leaders, including PM Modi, expressed relief and condemned the violence.

articles.headline: Modi hails India’s strides in nuclear, wind energy
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi called the criticality achievement in Kalpakkam's fast breeder nuclear reactor a "historic milestone" for India's nuclear energy. During 'Mann ki Baat', he also highlighted India ranking fourth globally in wind energy capacity and urged public participation in Census 2027.

articles.headline: DNT enumeration in Census discussed at Chintan Shivir
articles.summary: The Social Justice Ministry's "Chintan Shivir" concluded, discussing the inclusion of denotified, nomadic, and semi-nomadic tribes (DNTs) in Census 2027. DNT communities advocate for a separate column to account for their population, aiming for recognition similar to Scheduled Castes and Tribes.

articles.headline: Safe highway travel part of right to life: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court declared the right to safe passage on highways, protected from accidents due to administrative or infrastructural gaps, as a fundamental right to life under Article 21. It directed the prohibition of new dhaba construction within highway right-of-way and regulated parking.

articles.headline: Man opens fire in Dwarka, kills youth, injures friend
articles.summary: A 21-year-old was killed and a friend injured in Dwarka after a man, identified as Neeraj, opened fire during an altercation. The victim's family claims Neeraj is a police constable and hurled casteist slurs, but police ruled out caste as a motive, calling it an "impulsive act."

articles.headline: Implement Heat Action Plan, focus on Delhi's thermal hotspots: CM
articles.summary: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta directed officials to implement the Heat Action Plan (HAP) 2026, identifying high-risk "thermal hotspots" using satellite data. Special measures for these areas include increased ORS stock, water tankers, and restricting outdoor work during peak heat. The IMD issued a yellow alert for Monday.

articles.headline: NCR hospitals report surge in heat-related illnesses, OPD cases
