articles.summary: Hospitals in the National Capital Region (NCR) are seeing a sharp increase in heat-related illnesses, with OPDs reporting higher patient footfall. Symptoms include dehydration, exhaustion, dizziness. Vulnerable groups are the elderly, children, and outdoor workers. Doctors note worsening pre-existing conditions and respiratory issues.

articles.headline: TB patients without documents miss aid in Delhi
articles.summary: Homeless tuberculosis patients in Delhi struggle to access the Centre’s Nikshay Poshan Yojana (₹1,000/month) due to lacking Aadhaar cards, bank accounts, or mobile numbers. An NGO's PIL in Delhi HC highlighted that many malnourished patients, vital for recovery, miss this support.

articles.headline: Census 2027 survey faces heat, locked door challenges in Delhi
articles.summary: Enumerators for Census 2027 in New Delhi face low public awareness, non-cooperation, and hostile weather. The afternoon survey timing is punishing, and residents are often unavailable or unwilling to open doors, with gated colonies posing restricted access challenges.

articles.headline: Delhi Assembly to discuss women’s reservation tomorrow
articles.summary: The Delhi Assembly will hold a one-day special session on Tuesday to discuss women’s reservation, following the defeat of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill, 2026, in the Lok Sabha. CM Rekha Gupta accused the Opposition of "betraying" women; Opposition demands immediate 33% implementation.

articles.headline: Officials to face action for files pending over a month: Bihar CM
articles.summary: Bihar Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary warned of strict action against officials found holding up files for over a month, stating the NDA government aims to ensure basic amenities reach all households.

articles.headline: Former BJD MP, ex-MLA husband quit party in Odisha
articles.summary: In Odisha’s Bhadrak Lok Sabha constituency, former BJD MP Manjulata Mandal and her husband, former Dhamnagar MLA Muktikanta Mandal, resigned from the Biju Janata Dal (BJD), along with other leaders. They were suspended for "anti-party activities" on April 6.

articles.headline: SP, BJP exchange barbs over girl’s death in Ghazipur
articles.summary: Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav attacked the U.P. government over the recovery of a teenage girl’s body in Ghazipur, alleging attempts to "change statements" and harassment of the family. The BJP dismissed this as "divisive politics." An FIR is registered, and the main accused arrested.

articles.headline: Summer heat causes income shock for gig workers
articles.summary: India’s gig and delivery workers face significant income loss during intensifying heatwaves, as current heat action plans primarily focus on health, not economic consequences. This vulnerable workforce lacks paid leave or "work from home" options, leading to income volatility.

articles.headline: Gujarat civic polls see up to 66% turnout amid heat
articles.summary: Gujarat local body elections saw between 45% and 66% voter turnout despite intense heat. Polling for municipal corporations was lower than other local bodies. Three deaths were reported on polling day, including an AAP candidate.

articles.headline: Railway overbridge collapses in Visakhapatnam, 10 injured
articles.summary: Ten workers were seriously injured when a portion of an under-construction railway overbridge collapsed at Vadlapudi in Visakhapatnam, Andhra Pradesh. The bridge connects Auto Nagar and Duvvada. Doctors reported stable conditions for the injured.

articles.headline: Fadnavis backs ex-CJI Gavai amid Dhirendra Shastri meeting row
articles.summary: Maharashtra CM Devendra Fadnavis supported former CJI B.R. Gavai, who faced criticism for meeting Dhirendra Shastri. Social activist Shyam Manav criticized Gavai for invoking Ambedkar's name while supporting Shastri. Fadnavis dismissed Manav's comments as publicity-seeking.

articles.headline: Rajasthan as ‘living lab’ for farm tech firms: CM
articles.summary: Rajasthan Chief Minister Bhajan Lal Sharma invited agricultural technology companies to use the state as a "living lab" due to its crop diversity. He highlighted investment opportunities in food processing, cold chains, organic farming, and drone-based agriculture at an investor meet in Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Mumbai-Pune Expressway 'missing link' opens May 1
articles.summary: The "missing link" on the Mumbai-Pune Expressway, connecting Khopoli to Kusgaon, will open on May 1, Maharashtra Day. The project reduces travel distance to 13.3 km and travel time by half an hour, aiming to ease congestion at Khandala Ghat.

articles.headline: Telangana CM seeks refresh of grievance redress app Prajavani
articles.summary: Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy directed officials to strengthen "Prajavani," the State's grievance redress platform, by decentralizing the system to mandal and revenue division levels for easier public access and speedy resolution of complaints.

articles.headline: Visually challenged trainee judge gets inclusive campus
articles.summary: Thanya Nathan C., Kerala’s first visually challenged trainee judge, has an inclusive hostel room at the Kerala Judicial Academy with grab bars and modified bathroom. The campus has ramps and tactile paving, and fellow trainees will assist her, aiming for a fully inclusive environment.

articles.headline: Karnataka Minister on life support, may need lung transplant
articles.summary: Karnataka Minister for Planning and Statistics D. Sudhakar is on life support at KIMS Hospitals, Bengaluru, due to a lung infection. Doctors stated he may be considered for a lung transplant if his condition does not show significant improvement.

articles.headline: Upgraded SARPA platform to boost snakebite care in Kerala
articles.summary: Kerala Forest Department will launch an upgraded SARPA (Snake Awareness, Rescue and Protection App) platform to strengthen emergency response and reduce snakebite fatalities, amid a rise in cases. The new version will integrate medical professionals and experts for real-time guidance.

articles.headline: T.N. fossil assemblage dates to Holocene period: ZSI
articles.summary: A Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) assessment of a fossil assemblage at Panaiyur, Thoothukudi district, Tamil Nadu, indicates it dates back to the middle to late Holocene period, approximately 8,000 to 12,000 years ago, based on stratigraphic position and fossilisation.

articles.headline: Railways orders audit of signalling systems for safe operations
articles.summary: Indian Railways has ordered an audit of signalling systems nationwide to rectify deficiencies and improve reliability, following several fatal accidents where signal failure was suspected. The audit will cover installation quality, adherence to standards, and equipment condition.

articles.headline: SIR pauses India's electorate expansion, boosts turnout
articles.summary: India’s electoral roll, previously steadily expanding, is now shrinking due to a Special Intensive Revision (SIR) removing absent, shifted, dead, and duplicate names. This has led to record turnouts in States despite smaller electorates, strengthening accuracy but raising concerns about potential disenfranchisement.

articles.headline: Anthropic’s AI model Mythos raises cybersecurity concerns
articles.summary: Anthropic's AI model Mythos, designed to find software vulnerabilities, is facing scrutiny after unauthorized users accessed it, raising questions about safeguarding powerful AI. This incident, following an accidental leak of internal files, highlights the alarming asymmetry in cybersecurity, where attackers can exploit flaws faster than patches.

articles.headline: Experts seek data-led approach for public health risks
articles.summary: A webinar on "Public Health in India" highlighted the urgent need to strengthen preventive healthcare, build data-driven systems, and develop a skilled public health workforce. Experts stressed the shift from curative to prevention-led models, consistent policy, and better investment in health infrastructure.

articles.headline: Rahul plans Great Nicobar visit, clearance awaited
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi began a three-day visit to the Andaman and Nicobar Islands for a party convention and plans to visit Great Nicobar Island, awaiting administrative clearance. The party's focus is on the region.

articles.headline: West Asia war, Israel language derail BRICS joint statement
articles.summary: Heated debates on the West Asia war and language on Israel derailed a BRICS Deputy Foreign Ministers meeting in New Delhi, concluding without a joint statement. India reportedly attempted to "tone down" critical language on Israel and Palestine, opposed by other members, setting up difficult future negotiations.

articles.headline: Two women turn personal grief into poll contest for justice
articles.summary: BJP's Ratna Debnath and Left Front's Sabina Yasmin are contesting West Bengal Assembly elections to seek justice for their daughters killed in separate incidents. Their campaigns highlight issues of safety, governance, and State accountability, turning personal tragedies into political platforms.

articles.headline: Mamata seeks Bhabanipur support; Modi woos Matuas in Bengal
articles.summary: In the last leg of West Bengal campaigning, PM Modi focused on the Matua population, pledging citizenship via CAA, while CM Mamata Banerjee campaigned in her Bhabanipur constituency, accusing BJP of hooliganism and assuring no NRC implementation.

articles.headline: Drug menace key plank in Amit Shah’s Punjab campaign
articles.summary: Union Home Minister Amit Shah will frequently visit Punjab, targeting the drug menace as a key plank for the BJP's 2027 Assembly election campaign. The BJP, having brought seven AAP MPs to its side, aims to expand its influence and form its own government, promising to free Punjab from drugs within two years.

articles.headline: Kejriwal, Tejashwi accuse BJP of attacking Bengali culture
articles.summary: Arvind Kejriwal and Tejashwi Yadav campaigned for the Trinamool Congress in West Bengal, accusing the BJP of attacking Bengali culture and pride. Kejriwal alleged BJP destroyed Delhi's good work, while Yadav praised Mamata Banerjee's fight against PM Modi's influence.

articles.headline: Trinamool files case over ‘misuse of National Flag’
articles.summary: The Trinamool Congress lodged a complaint against BJP candidate Papia Adhikari for allegedly misusing the National Flag during her election campaign in the Tollygunge Assembly constituency in south Kolkata.

articles.headline: Rahul, Patnaik condole Raghu Rai’s passing
articles.summary: Congress leader Rahul Gandhi and BJD president Naveen Patnaik condoled the death of photographer Raghu Rai, praising his six-decade-long work. Gandhi stated Rai's lens "chronicled the soul of India," preserving the nation's memory, while Patnaik highlighted his frames as "living histories."

articles.headline: PM to launch ₹6,350 crore projects in Uttar Pradesh
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will visit Uttar Pradesh on April 28-29 to launch development projects worth approximately ₹6,350 crore in Varanasi. He will participate in a Mahila Sammelan, visit Kashi Vishawanath temple, and dedicate the Ganga Expressway to the nation in Hardoi.

articles.headline: N. Ireland police suspect dissident republicans in car bombing
articles.summary: Northern Ireland police suspect dissident republicans behind an overnight car bomb attack outside a police station near Belfast. First Minister Michelle O’Neill condemned the incident, stating those responsible "speak for absolutely no one."

articles.headline: Taiwan Minister hits out at China, says island will not bow to pressure
articles.summary: Taiwan's Foreign Minister Lin Chia-lung criticized China's pressure tactics, including influencing African nations to revoke travel permits for Taiwanese officials. He asserted that Taiwan will not "bow its head" despite these diplomatic challenges.

articles.headline: Syrian court begins first public trial of ousted Assad, allies
articles.summary: A Syrian court held the first hearing in the trial of ousted ruler Bashar al-Assad and senior figures from his government. Assad and his brother Maher will be tried in absentia, but former security official Atef Najib, a relative, was present.

articles.headline: Israel, Hezbollah accuse each other of Lebanon truce breach
articles.summary: Israel and Hezbollah traded accusations of violating the Lebanon ceasefire, with PM Benjamin Netanyahu stating the military was "vigorously" targeting the Iran-backed group. Both sides claimed new attacks in southern Lebanon, with Hezbollah vowing resistance to continued Israeli "occupation."

articles.headline: Iran’s Araghchi heads to Russia after Pakistan diplomatic push
articles.summary: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi visited Islamabad twice and Oman, and is now heading to Russia to meet Vladimir Putin, as mediators attempt to sustain peace talks between Tehran and the U.S. Trump, however, scrapped a planned trip by his negotiators to Islamabad, expressing skepticism over direct talks.

articles.headline: Sri Lanka seeks investors for loss-making Mattala airport
articles.summary: Sri Lanka invited Expressions of Interest for its loss-making Mattala International Airport, built with a nearly $200-million Chinese loan. The airport, dubbed the "world's emptiest," has accumulated $130 million in losses and seeks domestic and foreign investors for its operations.

articles.headline: CSK coach Fleming: "One step forward, two steps back"
articles.summary: Chennai Super Kings head coach Stephen Fleming acknowledged the team’s frustrating inconsistency after a home defeat to Gujarat Titans. He cited a challenging pitch and early conditions for poor stroke-making, but remained optimistic for upcoming games in the "toughest" IPL competition.

articles.headline: RCB holds aces against host DC in Kohli’s backyard
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru, confident after five wins, face Delhi Capitals at Arun Jaitley Stadium. DC, reeling from failing to defend 264 against PBKS, needs a win. RCB will rely on Virat Kohli (328 runs in seven innings) and Jacob Bethell, drafted in for injured Phil Salt.

articles.headline: Krunal Pandya redefining IPL finger-spin: Karthik
articles.summary: RCB batting coach Dinesh Karthik praised Krunal Pandya for "redefining the rules of finger-spin" in IPL. Pandya's unique repertoire, including bouncers and block-hole deliveries, makes him effective in T20 cricket. He has taken eight wickets in seven matches for RCB this season.

articles.headline: Cummins praises Suryavanshi despite ton against SRH
articles.summary: Rajasthan Royals' Vaibhav Suryavanshi scored a stunning 36-ball hundred against Sunrisers Hyderabad, earning praise from SRH skipper Pat Cummins, who called him his "new favourite player." Despite Suryavanshi's blazing ton, Royals lost the IPL game.

articles.headline: Indian women revive hopes with win over Ukraine
articles.summary: The young Indian women's badminton team secured a commanding 4-1 victory over Ukraine in the Uber Cup, bouncing back from a narrow loss to Denmark. Unnati Hooda, Tanvi Sharma, and Devika Sihag won singles matches, while the doubles pair of Sindhu and Tanisha Crasto sealed the scoreline.

articles.headline: Kwizera blazes to TCS World 10K Bengaluru record
articles.summary: Rodrigue Kwizera set a new men's event record at the TCS World 10K Bengaluru, finishing in 27:31s, beating the previous mark from 2022. Florence Niyonkuru won the international women's title. Harmanjot Singh and Sanjivani Jadhav recorded the best Indian timings.

articles.headline: Ham wins Singapore Open after holding off John
articles.summary: Jeongwoo Ham of South Korea won the Singapore Open with a two-shot victory over Australia's Cameron John, securing a wire-to-wire win despite a double bogey. Both players also booked tickets to The Open Championship.

articles.headline: Deepti shines as India beats South Africa in T20I
articles.summary: Deepti Sharma's unbeaten 36 and maiden five-wicket haul (5/19) led India to a 14-run win over South Africa in the fourth T20I. Jemimah Rodrigues (43) and Richa Ghosh (34 n.o.) also contributed to India's total of 185/5.

articles.headline: BWF votes for new 15-point scoring system
articles.summary: The Badminton World Federation (BWF) voted to change its scoring system from best-of-three games to 21 points, to first to 15 points, effective January 2027. BWF president Patama Leeswadtrakul called it an an "important milestone" for the sport's future.

articles.headline: Atletico builds confidence ahead of Arsenal clash
articles.summary: Atletico Madrid defeated Athletic Bilbao 3-2 in LaLiga, with Alexander Sorloth scoring twice, building confidence before their Champions League semifinal against Arsenal. Antoine Griezmann equalized, and Sorloth secured the win, though Pablo Barrios's injury is a concern.

articles.headline: Abhay Singh breaks u-20 200m National record
articles.summary: Reliance's Abhay Singh broke the under-20 men’s 200m National record at the 24th u-20 Federation athletics competition in Tumakuru, finishing in 20.82s and bettering Shanmuga Srinivasan’s previous best of 21.02s set in 2021.

articles.headline: Uttar Pradesh wins men’s junior gymnastics title
articles.summary: Uttar Pradesh lifted the men’s junior team title in the National gymnastics championships held in Bhubaneswar.

articles.headline: Legendary Indian Photographer Raghu Rai Passes Away at 83
articles.summary: Legendary Indian photographer Raghu Rai, 83, passed away Sunday after battling cancer. A Padma Shri recipient and Henri Cartier-Bresson protege, he extensively documented India's socio-political life for over six decades, including the 1971 Bangladesh war and Bhopal gas tragedy. Celebrated globally, he shaped photojournalism, capturing the nation's heart, soul, and conscience with unmatched sensitivity.

articles.headline: PM Modi Urges Participation in Secure, Digital 2027 Census
articles.summary: PM Modi urged active participation in the 2027 Census, assuring data security and confidentiality. The world's largest census will use digital forms for the first time, enabling self-enumeration 15 days prior to official visits. This data, highlighted in his Mann Ki Baat address, is crucial for India's infrastructure and welfare planning.

articles.headline: India, New Zealand Sign Landmark FTA to Double Trade by 2030
articles.summary: India and New Zealand are signing a Free Trade Agreement today after ten years of talks. The deal aims to double bilateral trade to $5 billion by 2030, eliminating 100% tariffs on India's exports like textiles and gems, and reducing/removing on 95% of NZ imports, while India excludes sensitive dairy. Commerce Minister Goyal concludes talks, with NZ seeking better access for sheep meat, wool, and fruit.

articles.headline: Sabastian Sawe Shatters Two-Hour Marathon Barrier in London
articles.summary: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe made history by winning the London Marathon in 1:59:30, officially breaking the two-hour barrier in a competitive race. This achievement shattered the previous world record by 65 seconds. Ethiopia's Yomif Kejelcha also finished under two hours, while Tigst Assefa broke the women's world record, aided by new 'super-shoes' and ideal conditions.

articles.headline: Zelenskyy Accuses Russia of Nuclear Terrorism on Chernobyl Anniversary
articles.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy accused Russia of "nuclear terrorism" on the 40th Chernobyl anniversary, following drone attacks near decommissioned nuclear sites that killed five. He warned Russian military activity, including drones regularly passing over Chernobyl and a hit on its protective shell last year, threatens Europe's nuclear safety, calling for an international security zone around all facilities.

articles.headline: AAP Petitions RS Chairman to Disqualify Seven Defecting MPs
articles.summary: AAP petitioned Rajya Sabha Chairman C.P. Radhakrishnan to disqualify seven MPs who defected to the BJP, citing the anti-defection law. AAP argues they violated party whip rules and voluntarily quit, asserting the "original party" didn't merge, calling it a "betrayal". Six of the MPs are from Punjab, where AAP protested, burning effigies and demanding membership cancellation.

articles.headline: Trump Safe After White House Dinner Shooting; Suspect Arrested
articles.summary: President Donald Trump was rushed from the White House Correspondents' Association dinner after 31-year-old Cole Tomas Allen fired a shotgun at a Secret Service agent (saved by vest) at a checkpoint. Acting AG Todd Blanche stated Trump and his administration were likely targets. Trump believes this is his third assassination attempt since 2024, amid US political polarization. World leaders condemned the attack.

articles.headline: Scaled-Down Thrissur Pooram Held Amidst Grief, Heat and Rituals
articles.summary: The Thrissur Pooram festival was held in a subdued format on Sunday, drawing thousands despite scorching heat and recent fireworks blast that killed 15. The event focused on traditional rituals, including caparisoned elephants from Paramekkavu and Thiruvambady, and percussion, but high-decibel fireworks and Kudamattam were curtailed as a tribute to the lives lost, signifying the symbolic meeting of deities.

articles.headline: NTPC Plans Two 700 MW Nuclear Units in Bihar's Banka District
articles.summary: State-owned NTPC plans two 700 MW nuclear power units in Bihar's Banka district, a ₹25,000 crore project requiring 1,000 acres. A feasibility study is underway, with Bihar assuring full support and water. This is part of NTPC's energy diversification strategy for ~30GW nuclear projects, aligning with India's 100GW nuclear capacity goal by 2047.

articles.headline: Union Govt. to Resume Ladakh Statehood, Sixth Schedule Talks on May 22
articles.summary: The Union Home Ministry will resume talks with Leh and Kargil civil society bodies on May 22 regarding demands for Ladakh's statehood and Sixth Schedule inclusion. Announced by Ladakh LG Vinai Kumar Saxena ahead of Home Minister Amit Shah's visit, the dialogue aims for substantive engagement through a high-powered committee to address local aspirations.

articles.headline: US-Iran Peace Hopes Fade as Trump Cancels Diplomatic Mission
articles.summary: Hopes for peace in the US-Israeli conflict with Iran faded as President Trump cancelled a diplomatic mission, stating Washington was open to talks only by phone and an Iranian offer was inadequate. Iran's Foreign Minister continued shuttle diplomacy, but the absence of US counterparts signals a stalemate as the war nears two months. Fighting also resumed between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon.

articles.headline: Swiss Flight Evacuated at Delhi Airport After Engine Failure During Take-off
articles.summary: A Swiss International Air Lines flight to Zurich was evacuated at Delhi airport early Sunday after an engine malfunction, possibly a fire near the landing gear, during take-off. All 228 passengers and four infants exited safely via emergency slides. Six passengers and a crew member sustained minor injuries. The Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau will probe the Airbus A330 incident.

articles.headline: HPCL: Leak Suspected Cause of Fire at Rajasthan Refinery
articles.summary: HPCL suspects a leak from a pressure gauge tapping point caused a fire at its ₹79,450 crore joint venture refinery's crude distillation unit in Rajasthan on April 20, just before its inauguration. Restoration will take 3-4 weeks, with the CDU restarting by late May, leading to the postponement of the dedication ceremony.

articles.headline: IBBI Panel Recommends Project-Wise Resolution for Realty Insolvencies
articles.summary: An IBBI committee recommended 155 ways to streamline real estate bankruptcies, including project-wise resolution for healthy projects and excluding completed units. It suggested raising the IBC filing threshold to ₹5 crore and exploring the SWAMIH fund for distressed housing. This aims to protect homebuyers, improve confidence, and ensure independent resolution, reserving corporate bankruptcy for fraud.

articles.headline: Data Reveals Steep Road, Pay Gap for Women in India's Boardrooms
articles.summary: Official data reveals women hold only 20.8% of board positions in 3,597 listed companies, dropping to 26% for the 18-30 age group, indicating a crucial pipeline gap. A significant pay gap exists, with male directors earning 3.6 times more, and almost two-thirds of companies lack female key managerial personnel.

articles.headline: India's Peak Power Demand Hits Record 256GW Amidst Heatwave
articles.summary: India's peak power demand hit an all-time high of 256.11 GW on Saturday, surpassing the 2024 peak of 251 GW. Driven by intense heatwaves (40-46°C) across northwest and central regions and early summer onset, this challenges the power system weeks before peak season. Discoms are urged to ensure network slack and reserves.

articles.headline: India Aims to Cut Onion Wastage for Price Stabilization
articles.summary: India aims to reduce wastage of its 200,000 tonnes of procured onions this season, targeting a 75% recovery rate (up from 70%) under the Price Stabilisation Fund. This seeks to ensure better quality, stabilize retail prices, and efficiently utilize funds, especially given a predicted below-normal monsoon impacting kharif onions and farmers' concerns over crashing prices.

articles.headline: Online Fashion Apps Shift to Premium, Luxury Segment for Growth
articles.summary: Indian online fashion and beauty platforms like Myntra, Ajio, Amazon, and Nykaa are undergoing a "great reset," shifting from discount-led models to focus on premium and luxury products. This aims for better margins, responds to evolving demand from tier II/III cities, and taps into India’s lifestyle market (projected $210 billion by 2028), despite consumers expecting deals.

articles.headline: Motorcycle Makers Shift Focus to Premium Models for Growth
articles.summary: Major motorcycle manufacturers like Hero MotoCorp, Bajaj Auto, and TVS Motor are intensifying their focus on premium models (above 125cc). This segment grew 15% to 3.5 million units in FY26, outpacing entry-level sales (3%), and targets affluent riders. This strategy aims to offset risks from uncertain rural demand amid West Asia war and erratic monsoon forecasts.

articles.headline: Pakistan Scrambles to Reignite Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks
articles.summary: President Trump cancelled US envoys' Pakistan trip for Iran talks, deeming an offer inadequate, leaving him with tough choices. Pakistan is scrambling to reignite talks, as the diplomatic deadlock persists over the US blockade, Strait of Hormuz, and Iran’s nuclear program. Iran insists on the blockade's lifting before new talks, with Oman supporting a toll mechanism.

articles.headline: India, NZ Sign FTA Today; Enforcement Expected Later This Year
articles.summary: India and New Zealand are signing a Free Trade Agreement today, expected to take effect later this year after New Zealand's parliamentary scrutiny. PM Christopher Luxon hailed it as a "once in a generation agreement," offering Kiwi exporters "unprecedented access" to India's 1.4 billion market, further strengthening bilateral trade ties.

articles.headline: Councillor's Family: 3 Killed at UP Birthday Party Shooting
articles.summary: Three members of a councillor's family were shot dead at a birthday party in a Bulandshahr, UP gym after an argument escalated. The accused, Jeetu Saini, fled. Police noted alcohol as a factor and are investigating old rivalries and political links, with six teams formed to arrest the suspect.

articles.headline: Minister's Son Wedding Planned in Rajaji Tiger Reserve, 2 Booked
articles.summary: The Uttarakhand forest department booked two temple committee office-bearers for planning state cabinet minister Khajan Dass's son's elaborate wedding inside Rajaji Tiger Reserve's core zone. After controversy, the minister moved the function outside the reserve. A probe under the Wildlife Protection Act is underway.

articles.headline: Centre Aims to End Northeast Insurgency by 2029
articles.summary: The Centre will intensify efforts to end insurgency in the Northeast by 2029, redeploying forces from former LWE areas to states like Manipur, Assam, Arunachal Pradesh, and Nagaland. This follows a significant decline in LWE violence. Manipur, with 226 cases in 2024, is a primary focus for this new strategy.

articles.headline: Pawan Khera Moves SC Against Bail Rejection in CM Sarma's Wife Case
articles.summary: Congress leader Pawan Khera moved the Supreme Court, challenging the Gauhati High Court's rejection of his anticipatory bail. This relates to allegations he made against Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife concerning multiple passports and undisclosed properties abroad, for which the High Court deemed custodial interrogation necessary.

articles.headline: Karnataka Dy CM Shivakumar Defers Leadership Decision to Congress High Command
articles.summary: Karnataka Deputy CM DK Shivakumar stated that the Congress high command would decide on the state's leadership, a decision he and CM Siddaramaiah would accept. This comes amidst speculation of a reshuffle after upcoming election results. Shivakumar also warned against flex banners for his birthday and discussed submitting urban development projects to the Centre.

articles.headline: AP CM Naidu Orders Restoration of Fuel Supply by Monday After Shortages
articles.summary: Andhra Pradesh CM N. Chandrababu Naidu directed officials and oil companies to restore adequate fuel supply by Monday. A sudden demand surge, partly from bulk purchases and commercial consumers shifting to retail, caused shortages, leading to over 400 (10%) fuel stations closing. Naidu warned against deliberate disruptions.

articles.headline: NCSC Seeks Report on Mamata's Alleged Derogatory Remark on SCs
articles.summary: The National Commission for Scheduled Castes (NCSC) took suo motu cognisance of a video allegedly showing West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee using "derogatory" language against the SC community voters during an election rally. NCSC has requested a detailed report from the state chief secretary and DGP within three days.

articles.headline: Explosive Device Kills 20, Injures 30 on Bus in Colombia
articles.summary: An explosive device detonated on a bus in Cajibio, southwestern Colombia, on Saturday, killing 20 people and injuring 30. The Army chief called it a "terrorist act." This incident is part of escalating violence, with at least 26 incidents in the region over two days, linked to illegal armed groups and drug trafficking.

articles.headline: Mali Defence Minister Killed Amid Coordinated Rebel, Jihadist Attacks
articles.summary: Mali's military rulers face a security crisis after Defence Minister General Sadio Camara and two grandchildren were killed in a car bomb attack on his home Saturday. This occurred during coordinated nationwide attacks by jihadist fighters and Tuareg separatist rebels, with a key northern town reportedly seized. These are the worst attacks since the junta seized power in 2020.

articles.headline: Mohammedan SC Fights Back for 2-2 Draw with SC Delhi in ISL
articles.summary: Mohammedan Sporting Club came from behind twice to secure a 2-2 draw against Sporting Club Delhi in their ISL match. Matija Babovic scored an early penalty for Delhi, while Thokchom Adison Singh and Hira Mondal netted for Mohammedan SC to level the scores.

articles.headline: Gujarat Titans Beat CSK by 8 Wickets, Led by Sai Sudharsan's 87
articles.summary: Gujarat Titans defeated Chennai Super Kings by eight wickets with 20 balls to spare. Titans' bowlers, led by Kagiso Rabada (3/25) and Mohammed Siraj (1/23), restricted CSK to 158/7, despite Ruturaj Gaikwad's 74. B. Sai Sudharsan's unbeaten 87 off 46 balls, supported by Jos Buttler's 39, then guided Titans to victory in 16.4 overs.

articles.headline: KKR Wins Super Over Thriller Against LSG; Rinku, Narine Star
articles.summary: Kolkata Knight Riders (KKR) won the first IPL 2026 Super Over thriller against Lucknow Super Giants (LSG). Sunil Narine took two Super Over wickets, restricting LSG to one run. Rinku Singh, who earlier scored an unbeaten 83 off 51 balls in regular play, then secured KKR's win with a boundary. Mohsin Khan took 5/23 for LSG, and Angkrish Raghuvanshi was dismissed for obstructing the field.
